description: AI meditation composition rules - 4-phase blueprint with finite focus modules (IM: I AM Mantra, NF: Nostril Focus, MV: Morning Key Moments / Morning Gratitude fractional modules). Theme tags (morning, evening, noon, night, sleep, gratitude) from prompt, LLM extract, client context, and Explore time-of-day bias INT_FRAC greeting family and, when focus minutes > 0, may select MV_KM_FRAC or MV_GR_FRAC instead of IM_FRAC unless the user chose IM or NF. User IM/NF always wins. For AI sessions 1-4 minutes, omit INT_FRAC; for 5+ minutes, include INT_FRAC@start. Do not include Gentle Bell (GB) at the end unless the user explicitly asks for a closing bell; users can add GB in the Timer builder.

priority_detection.description: When user explicitly requests a specific exercise, give it 50% of session time

breathing.allocation: 50% of session duration (breath phase via PB_FRAC, up to 5 minutes)

i_am_mantra.allocation: 50% of session duration (uses IM_FRAC fractional module)

when_priority_detected.action: Give priority exercise 50% of total session time
when_priority_detected.example_10m_bodyscan: INT_FRAC@start, PB_FRAC@1 (durationMinutes=1), BS5@2, IM_FRAC@7 — no GB unless user asks
when_priority_detected.example_3m_short_ai: PB_FRAC@0 (durationMinutes=2), IM_FRAC@2 — no INT_FRAC for AI sessions under 5 minutes; omit GB unless user asks

finite_exercise.description: Full-duration guided audio. The audio file IS the exercise.
finite_exercise.behavior: Occupies its full duration in the session timeline. No additional quiet time needed.

trigger_cue.description: Short audio trigger that instructs user to BEGIN a practice. User continues in silence.
trigger_cue.time_allocation: Cue audio + remaining phase time as quiet practice
trigger_cue.behavior: After audio plays, user continues the practice silently until next module.
trigger_cue.audio_duration_note: Actual duration loaded from cues_models.json via CueManager

instant_cue.description: Brief sound marker with no time allocation needed.
instant_cue.behavior: Instant audio marker, does not occupy timeline space.

GB.note: Optional for AI: include only when the user explicitly requests a bell at the end. Default AI sessions omit GB.

short_session_rules.description: Rules for sessions 1-6 minutes. Simpler structure than 4-phase. AI sessions 1-4 minutes: do not use INT_FRAC; first fractional cue at practice minute 0 (trigger "0" or "start" as appropriate). AI 5+ minutes: include INT_FRAC@start as in longer examples.

1min.structure: One relaxation or focus fractional at minute 0 (no INT_FRAC for AI under 5 min)
1min.notes: Introduction-only 1m AI: omit INT_FRAC; omit GB unless user asks for a closing bell.

2-3min.structure: ONE relaxation (PB or BS) at minute 0 (no INT_FRAC for AI under 5 min)
2-3min.notes: Choose PB or BS based on prompt, not both. Omit GB unless user asks.

4min.structure: PB + BS at minute 0 onward (no INT_FRAC for AI under 5 min; no focus module)
4min.notes: Both breathing and body scan, but no focus module. Omit GB unless user asks.

5-6min.notes: ONE fractional focus module (IM_FRAC or NF_FRAC randomly, OH for sleep, explicit request overrides)

short_session_rules.gb_rule: Omit GB (Gentle Bell) in default AI sessions. Include GB at trigger 'end' only if the user explicitly asks for a bell or chime at the end. Timer / custom builder still offers GB as an optional cue.

composition_algorithm.description: 4-phase blueprint for sessions > 6 minutes. Fills 100% of session time.

settle_in.note: INT_FRAC applies when the session uses the fractional intro row (AI 5+ minutes). AI 1-4 minutes omit INT_FRAC; first content begins at practice minute 0.

focus.purpose: Guided focus practice (I AM mantra or nostril focus) or emotional focus
focus.note: IM and NF are finite exercises (1-10m guided audio). Use only ONE per session unless user explicitly requests both. OH remains a trigger cue for sleep/emotional sessions.

visualization.note: OH is NOT a visualization cue - use only VC or RT

closing.note: Default: no closing cue module. Optional GB only if the user explicitly requests an end bell.

steps.details: For AI sessions 5+ minutes and the long-session blueprint: add INT_FRAC at trigger 'start'. For AI sessions 1-4 minutes: omit INT_FRAC; place the first content module at practice minute 0.
PB gets 40% of relaxation time. relaxation_time = relaxation_end - 1. PB_duration = round(relaxation_time * 0.4), capped at 5.

steps.action: Add body scan (BS) in relaxation phase
steps.details: BS gets remaining relaxation time. BS_duration = relaxation_end - 1 - PB_duration, capped at 10.

steps.action: Add focus module (IM or NF) in focus phase
steps.details: Select ONE focus module: IM (I AM Mantra) or NF (Nostril Focus), randomly unless user requested a specific one. Duration = focus_end - relaxation_end, capped at 10, min 1 (NF) or 2 (IM). Both are finite exercises (guided audio fills exact duration).
Add VC (morning) or RT (evening) at focus_end. Audio plays for 2 min, user practices silently until session end.
Do not add GB unless the user explicitly asks for a gentle bell or chime at the end. Otherwise the session ends after the last guided module.

session_templates.description: Pre-calculated examples. Short sessions (1-6m) use simplified structure, long sessions (>6m) use 4-phase blueprint.

1min.composition: PB_FRAC(0-1, 1m) — AI 1-4m: no INT_FRAC; no GB unless user asks
1min.notes: AI under 5 min: omit INT_FRAC. Omit GB unless user asks for a closing bell.

2min.notes: Could also use BS2@0 instead of PB_FRAC

3min.notes: Could also use BS3@0 instead of PB_FRAC

5min.notes: ONE fractional focus module (IM_FRAC default, VC/RT for specific session types)

6min.notes: ONE fractional focus module (IM_FRAC default, VC/RT for specific session types)

focus_phase.description: IM (I AM Mantra) or NF (Nostril Focus) as finite focus exercise (randomly chosen). OH for sleep/emotional sessions. Only ONE focus module per session unless user explicitly requests both.

visualization_phase.description: VC for intentions/goals, RT for evening reflection. OH is NOT a visualization cue.

introduction_selection.description: For AI sessions 5+ minutes, use INT_FRAC (Intro) at trigger start. For AI sessions 1-4 minutes, omit INT_FRAC; first guided content uses trigger 0 or start as appropriate.

rules: Omit GB unless the user explicitly asks for a closing bell
For AI sessions 5+ minutes use INT_FRAC (Intro) at start; for 1-4 minutes omit INT_FRAC
Use body scans to release tension

strict_rules.rule: AI sessions 1-4 minutes: do not include INT_FRAC. AI sessions 5+ minutes: include INT_FRAC at trigger 'start'.
When INT_FRAC is present, it must be at trigger 'start' (never a numeric minute).
Do not include GB in AI-generated meditations unless the user explicitly requests a bell or chime at the end. If included, GB must use trigger 'end'.
Modules must NOT overlap - each module's end time must be <= next module's start time
Modules must fill 100% of the session duration using the 4-phase structure
Sessions > 6 min must have one finite focus module (IM or NF) and one visualization trigger cue (VC/RT)
Trigger values must be 'start', 'end', or a valid minute number (string)

When IM_FRAC or NF_FRAC has durationMinutes equal to the full session length (entire practice is that fractional module), use trigger 'start' so it begins at meditation clock 00:00 after any INT_FRAC intro, or at 00:00 when INT_FRAC is omitted (AI 1-4 minutes) — not minute 1 unless another module occupies minute 0. Minute numbers are practice-minute positions; a full-session focus module is not 'one minute into practice'.
Always include a background sound and binaural beat unless user explicitly asks for silence

background_sound_guidelines.description: Guidelines for selecting appropriate background sounds

json_output_format.description: The exact JSON format the AI must return. Omit GB (Gentle Bell) from cues unless the user explicitly requests an end bell; Timer still allows adding GB manually.

template.title: Short descriptive title
template.description: One sentence description of the meditation

example.title: Morning Focus Meditation
example.description: A 10-minute session with breathwork, body scan, I AM mantra, and vision clarity.
